pi_items.source: Task setup script (setup_task.sh) creates this directory at line 8 and exports result script validates this path at line 6. File path is conventional for LibreCAD user drawings.

Task description and verifier.py lines 18-24 confirm these layer names are required. These are arbitrary layer names that can be created in any DXF file and are not tied to specific LibreCAD capabilities.

Verified against LibreCAD official GitHub repository at https://github.com/LibreCAD/LibreCAD/tree/master/librecad/support/patterns. ANSI31 exists as ansi31.dxf in LibreCAD's pattern directory. Confirmed by official LibreCAD documentation at https://docs.librecad.org/en/latest/appx/hatches.html which lists ANSI31 as an available pattern.

Verified against LibreCAD official GitHub repository at https://github.com/LibreCAD/LibreCAD/tree/master/librecad/support/patterns. Only ANSI31 exists in LibreCAD's standard pattern directory. No ansi37.dxf file found. ANSI37 is mentioned in general CAD contexts (e.g., https://forums.autodesk.com/t5/autocad-forum/what-material-ansi31-through-ansi38-hatch-stand-for/td-p/2528305) but is not available in LibreCAD. This claim is CONTRADICTED by actual LibreCAD capabilities.

Verified against LibreCAD official GitHub repository at https://github.com/LibreCAD/LibreCAD/tree/master/librecad/support/patterns. None of the three claimed patterns exist in LibreCAD: (1) AR-SAND: While AutoCAD has ar-sand pattern (see https://forums.autodesk.com/t5/autocad-forum/autocad-plotting-ar-sand-hatch-dots-at-inconsistent-sizes/td-p/8572763), LibreCAD does not include ar-sand.dxf. Available AR-* patterns in LibreCAD are: ar-b816, ar-b816c, ar-b88, ar-brelm, ar-brstd, ar-conc, ar-hbone, ar-parq1, ar-roof, ar-rshke. (2) ANSI33: Does not exist in LibreCAD. Only ANSI31 is available. (3) DOTS: Not a standard LibreCAD pattern. This claim is CONTRADICTED by actual LibreCAD capabilities.

Task description states 'Add text labels for each context' with these specific soil layer names. Verifier.py lines 148-165 confirm these text strings should be present in the DXF file. These are arbitrary text strings that can be added to any DXF file.
Task description explicitly states 'Draw a trench outline (4m wide x 2m deep)' and task.json metadata specifies bounds from 0 to 4 on X-axis. Verifier checks bounds at lines 31-36 of task.json.
Task description explicitly states 'Draw a trench outline (4m wide x 2m deep)' indicating 2m depth. Task.json metadata specifies bounds from 0 to 2 on Y-axis.

privileged_info_summary: The archaeological_section task is a synthetic LibreCAD drawing exercise where users create a digitized soil layer section. Verified facts: (1) The output path /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/section_drawing.dxf is standard for LibreCAD user files. (2) Required layers BOUNDARIES, HATCH-TOPSOIL, HATCH-CLAY, HATCH-GRAVEL, and TEXT are arbitrary naming conventions that can be created in any DXF file. (3) ANSI31 hatch pattern for HATCH-TOPSOIL is verified to exist in LibreCAD. (4) The bounds specifications (4m wide x 2m deep, coordinates 0-4 x 0-2) are correctly matched to the task description. (5) Required text labels TOPSOIL, CLAY, GRAVEL are arbitrary strings creatable in any DXF file. CRITICAL: The hatch pattern claims contain contradictions with LibreCAD's actual available patterns: ANSI37 and ANSI33 do not exist in LibreCAD (only ANSI31 is available as an ANSI pattern), and AR-SAND does not exist as a built-in LibreCAD pattern (though similar AR-* patterns like ar-conc exist). DOTS is also not an available LibreCAD pattern. An agent attempting to complete this task would be unable to find these non-existent hatch patterns in LibreCAD's UI and would fail the verification if it tried to apply them as specified.
